prerequisites: A Customer.io account with access to the workspace you want to sync.
An App API key generated under Account Settings > API Credentials. The key is shown only once at creation, so copy it immediately.
Know your account region (US or EU), since it determines the API base URL the connector calls.

authMethods.name: App API key (Bearer token)
authMethods.summary: The connector authenticates with the App API key supplied in the app_api_key secret field. Customer.io's App API uses bearer authorization, sending the key as Authorization: Bearer <App API Key> on each request. Generate the key in the Customer.io UI under Account Settings > API Credentials for the target workspace.

setupSteps.title: Create an App API key in Customer.io
setupSteps.body: In Customer.io, open Account Settings > API Credentials and add a new App API key for the workspace you want to sync. App API keys are shown only once when created and are stored as hashed values, so copy the key right away and keep it secret.

setupSteps.title: Provide the App API key
setupSteps.body: Enter the copied key in the connector's app_api_key field. This is the only required secret. The connector sends it as a bearer token (Authorization: Bearer <key>) to the Customer.io App API.

setupSteps.title: Select your account region
setupSteps.body: Set the optional region field to match where your Customer.io account stores data: US or EU. If you do not specify a region, Customer.io assumes the US region. EU accounts append -eu to the API subdomain. Choosing the wrong region can cause authentication or routing errors.

setupSteps.title: Set an optional start date
setupSteps.body: Use the optional start_date field to define the earliest point from which records are synced for incremental streams. Leave it blank to sync from the connector's default range.

sources.title: About Customer.io's APIs (App API uses bearer authorization)
Manage your API credentials (creating App API keys)
Account regions and data centers (US/EU base URLs)
